# DOSSIER: LIAM OTTLEY

> **Version:** 1.0.0
> **Created:** 2026-03-12
> **Last Updated:** 2026-03-12
> **Sources Processed:** 3 (LO-001, LO-002, LO-003)
> **DNA Elements:** 27 (7 philosophies + 5 mental models + 8 heuristics + 4 frameworks + 3 methodologies)

---

## WHO IS LIAM OTTLEY

Liam Ottley is a New Zealand-born, Cape Town-based entrepreneur with 7 years of business
experience and 3 years focused specifically on AI. He runs four companies: Morningside AI
(AI consulting and workforce building), a personal brand (YouTube, education), an education
business (courses on AI implementation), and an AI SaaS product.

What makes Liam distinct from other AI educators is that he is a **practitioner, not a theorist**.
He builds and operates AI systems for his own businesses before teaching them. His most dramatic
proof point: generating **$1M NZD in 7 days** using his AI Operating System to research, create
content, build infrastructure, deliver, and collect payment — a demonstration of what a fully
operational AIOS makes possible.

He coined and popularized the term **AIOS (AI Operating System)** — a 5-layer architecture that
wraps around an existing business model to automate operations. His target: **60-70% automation
of repetitive tasks within 30 days** of implementation.

His tech stack is built on **Claude Code** as the core engine, with MCP servers for data
integration, skills for automation, cron for scheduling, and Telegram for mobile command.

---

## WHY RELEVANT TO MEGA BRAIN

Liam Ottley is uniquely relevant to Mega Brain because **Mega Brain IS an AIOS implementation**.
His 5-layer architecture maps directly to Mega Brain's architecture:

| Liam's Layer | Mega Brain Equivalent | Status |
|-------------|----------------------|--------|
| L1 Context | `CLAUDE.md` + `.claude/rules/` + `system/` | BUILT |
| L2 Data | MCP servers (`n8n-mcp`, `read-ai`, `mega-brain-knowledge`) | BUILT |
| L3 Intelligence | `core/intelligence/` (RAG, pipeline, knowledge graph) | BUILT |
| L4 Automate | `.claude/skills/` + `.claude/hooks/` + cron jobs | BUILT |
| L5 Build | Epic cycles, GSD workflow, development pipeline | BUILT |

Mega Brain has independently built almost exactly what Liam describes, which validates both
approaches. His frameworks provide useful **language and mental models** for describing what
Mega Brain already does, and identify potential gaps (mobile bridge, task audit formalization).

---

## KEY DIFFERENTIATORS

| Aspect | Liam's Approach | How It Differs |
|--------|----------------|----------------|
| **Core belief** | AIOS is the 3rd major shift (after Industrial Revolution, Internet) | Most AI educators treat AI as a tool, not a paradigm shift |
| **Unit of automation** | Departments, not tasks | Most focus on individual task automation |
| **Revenue model** | Dual: operate your AIOS + sell it to others ($3K-$10K+) | Most treat AI as cost center, not profit center |
| **Speed benchmark** | $1M in 7 days as proof of system capability | Most have no concrete speed benchmark |
| **Success metric** | Revenue per employee, not headcount | Contrarian to traditional scaling wisdom |
| **Implementation** | Claude Code-native (not ChatGPT, not custom code) | Aligned with Mega Brain's architecture choices |

| Source | Connection | DNA Reference |

| **Pedro Valerio** | Automation expertise + technical implementation. Pedro's automation heuristics complement Liam's automation targets (60-70%). Both are practitioners who build before teaching. | HEUR-LO-001 connects to Pedro Valerio's automation frameworks |

## SIGNATURE PHRASES

- "The Industrial Revolution changed how we MAKE. The Internet changed how we REACH. AIOS changes how we RUN."
- "This is not ChatGPT. This is an entirely different beast."
- "Revenue per employee is the new flex."
- "Within 30 days you should be automating 60 to 70 percent of your tasks."
- "Can you run your whole business from your phone for a day? That's the test."
- "Every automated task is time you never lose again. It compounds."
- "We're not automating tasks anymore. We're replacing entire departments with digital teams."
- "Context, Data, Intelligence, Automate, Build. In that order."
- "Day 1-2 research, Day 3-4 content, Day 5 infrastructure, Day 6 deliver, Day 7 collect. $1M in 7 days."
- "Package it, sell it. Your AIOS is also your product."

## DNA SUMMARY (27 Elements)

### L1 PHILOSOPHIES (7)

| ID | Name | Core Insight |
|----|------|-------------|
| FIL-LO-001 | AIOS Is The Third Major Shift | Industrial=MAKE, Internet=REACH, AIOS=RUN |
| FIL-LO-002 | Layers Not Leaps | AIOS built incrementally, each layer compounds |
| FIL-LO-003 | Bandwidth Is The Bottleneck | Invert 80/20 IN→ON to 20/80 |
| FIL-LO-004 | Not ChatGPT — Entirely Different Beast | AIOS is a system, not a chatbot |
| FIL-LO-005 | Revenue Per Employee Is The New Flex | Efficiency > headcount |
| FIL-LO-006 | Skills Are Transferable Modules | AIOS components = sellable IP |
| FIL-LO-007 | AI Workforces Replace Departments | Unit of automation = department |

### L2 MENTAL MODELS (5)

| ID | Name | Core Insight |
|----|------|-------------|
| MM-LO-001 | The Operator Trap | 80% IN / 20% ON → target 20/80 |
| MM-LO-002 | The Wrapper Model | Business as cube, AIOS wraps in 5 layers |
| MM-LO-003 | Compounding Automation | Every automated task compounds weekly |
| MM-LO-004 | Build or Enjoy | Freed bandwidth → choice: expand or freedom |
| MM-LO-005 | One-Person Billion Dollar Company | AI workforces = infinite capability |

### L3 HEURISTICS (8)

| ID | Name | Type | Core Insight |
|----|------|------|-------------|
| HEUR-LO-001 | 60-70% Automation Target | Numeric | Baseline within 30 days |
| HEUR-LO-002 | 7-Day Sprint Test | Qualitative | Idea to $1M in 7 days = system works |
| HEUR-LO-003 | Away-From-Desk Test | Qualitative | Run business from phone for a day |
| HEUR-LO-004 | Context Before Automation | Qualitative | Never automate without full context |
| HEUR-LO-005 | Daily Brief As First Milestone | Qualitative | Morning brief = first checkpoint |
| HEUR-LO-006 | Task Audit Before Automating | Qualitative | List, categorize, prioritize tasks |
| HEUR-LO-007 | 15% AI Decisions by 2028 | Numeric | Gartner prediction, we're already there |
| HEUR-LO-008 | Three KPIs of AIOS | Qualitative | Autonomy, automation %, rev/employee |

### L4 FRAMEWORKS (4)

| ID | Name | Core Structure |
|----|------|---------------|
| FW-LO-001 | 5-Layer AIOS Architecture | Context → Data → Intelligence → Automate → Build |
| FW-LO-002 | AI Workforce Architecture | Digital teams per department (mktg, sales, ops, creative) |
| FW-LO-003 | Task Audit Matrix | List → Categorize (Y/P/N) → Measure bandwidth → Prioritize |
| FW-LO-004 | Claude Code AIOS Stack | Claude Code + context + MCP + skills + cron + Telegram |

### L5 METHODOLOGIES (3)

| ID | Name | Steps |
|----|------|-------|
| MET-LO-001 | AIOS Implementation Sequence | 7 steps: Context → Data → Intelligence → Audit → Automate → Mobile → Build |
| MET-LO-002 | 7-Day Sprint Methodology | Research → Content → Infrastructure → Deliver → Collect = $1M |
| MET-LO-003 | AI Workforce Build Process | Define dept → Map roles → Build core → Coordinate → Test → Sell ($3K-$10K+) |
